risks: APIM Consumption tier does not support the rate-limit-by-key policy required for per-user rate limiting — a core requirement of the stated goal
disableLocalAuth is set to false on the Azure OpenAI resource, leaving API key authentication enabled alongside managed identity contrary to security best practices
publicNetworkAccess is Enabled on both Azure OpenAI and Cosmos DB with no private endpoints or VNet integration for network isolation
App Service Plan B1 (Basic) lacks autoscale, deployment slots, and availability zone support, limiting reliability and zero-downtime deployments
Cosmos DB serverless is single-region only with no zone redundancy, creating a single point of failure
APIM Consumption tier introduces cold-start latency of 1-2 seconds on idle and has a 10,000 requests/min gateway cap
correctionsRecommended: Upgrade APIM from Consumption to BasicV2 or StandardV2 tier to enable rate-limit-by-key policy for per-user throttling, or use the rate-limit policy as a temporary workaround
Set disableLocalAuth to true on the Azure OpenAI resource (Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts) to enforce managed-identity-only authentication
Add private endpoints for Azure OpenAI and Cosmos DB and disable public network access for production-grade network isolation
Upgrade App Service Plan from B1 to S1 or P0v3 when traffic requires autoscale, deployment slots, or availability zone support
Add resource tags across all resources for cost management, environment identification, and operational tracking
